title: Acute Myocardial Infarction Diagnosis
content: STEMI diagnosis requires: chest pain >20 min, ECG changes (ST elevation ≥1mm in ≥2 contiguous leads or new LBBB), troponin elevation. Key differentials: NSTEMI, unstable angina, aortic dissection, pulmonary embolism, pericarditis. Immediate management: aspirin 325mg, nitroglycerin, heparin, PCI within 90 minutes door-to-balloon time. GRACE score predicts 6-month mortality.

title: Heart Failure Classification
content: HFrEF (EF<40%): ACE-i/ARB, beta-blocker, aldosterone antagonist, SGLT2i. HFpEF (EF>50%): diuretics for symptom relief, treat comorbidities. NYHA Class I-IV guides treatment intensity. BNP >100 pg/mL or NT-proBNP >300 pg/mL suggests heart failure. Echo is gold standard. 6-minute walk test assesses functional capacity.

title: Lung Cancer Staging and Workup
content: NSCLC staging: IA-IVB based on TNM. PET-CT for staging, brain MRI for stage III-IV. Molecular testing: EGFR, ALK, ROS1, KRAS G12C, PD-L1 TPS mandatory for advanced disease. EGFR mutant: osimertinib first-line. ALK+: alectinib. PD-L1 ≥50%: pembrolizumab monotherapy. Liquid biopsy if tissue unavailable. Early stage: surgery or SBRT.

title: Cancer of Unknown Primary
content: CUP workup: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, PET-CT, IHC panel (CK7/CK20, TTF-1, CDX2, ER/PR, PSA, AFP, hCG, CD30). NGS molecular profiling can identify origin in 70% of cases. Poor prognosis subsets vs favorable subsets (peritoneal carcinomatosis in women, axillary node mets in women, midline germ cell syndrome). Empiric platinum-based chemo if no targetable mutation.

title: Stroke Diagnosis and Acute Management
content: FAST criteria: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call 911. CT head non-contrast to exclude hemorrhage. CT angiography for large vessel occlusion. IV tPA within 4.5 hours if no contraindications (hemorrhage, BP>185/110, anticoagulation, recent surgery). Mechanical thrombectomy up to 24 hours for LVO (NIHSS ≥6, ASPECTS ≥6). NIHSS score guides severity.

title: Seizure and Epilepsy Evaluation
content: First seizure workup: MRI brain with epilepsy protocol, EEG, metabolic panel (glucose, Na, Ca, Mg), LP if meningitis suspected. Status epilepticus: lorazepam 0.1mg/kg IV then fosphenytoin/levetiracetam then propofol/midazolam drip. Provoked vs unprovoked determines treatment. AED selection: focal epilepsy (levetiracetam, oxcarbazepine), generalized (valproate, lamotrigine).

title: Differential Diagnosis of Chest Pain
content: Cardiac: ACS, pericarditis, myocarditis, aortic dissection. Pulmonary: PE, pneumothorax, pleuritis, pneumonia. GI: GERD, esophageal spasm, peptic ulcer. MSK: costochondritis, rib fracture, muscle strain. Anxiety/panic disorder. Red flags: radiation to arm/jaw, diaphoresis, hemodynamic instability, pleuritic character, sudden onset tearing. Risk stratify with HEART score or TIMI score.

title: Fatigue Workup
content: CBC (anemia, leukemia), CMP (renal/hepatic disease), TSH (thyroid), fasting glucose (diabetes), ferritin, B12/folate, ESR/CRP (inflammatory), HIV, hepatitis panel. Consider: depression screen (PHQ-9), sleep apnea (STOP-BANG), cardiac (BNP, echo). Red flags: weight loss, night sweats, lymphadenopathy (malignancy), focal neuro signs. Chronic fatigue syndrome diagnosis of exclusion.

title: Medical Ethics Principles
content: Four principles: Autonomy (patient right to self-determination), Beneficence (act in patient best interest), Non-maleficence (do no harm), Justice (fair distribution of resources). Informed consent requires: disclosure, comprehension, voluntariness, capacity, decision. Capacity vs competence. Advance directives and surrogate decision-making. Futility criteria. Proportionality of intervention vs burden.

title: Cost-Benefit in Medical Decision Making
content: Cost-effectiveness analysis: cost per QALY (quality-adjusted life year). Threshold: $50,000-$150,000/QALY in US. Value-based care. Insurance coverage and formulary restrictions. Off-label use considerations. Compassionate use and expanded access programs. Patient financial toxicity is a real consideration. Shared decision making tools and decision aids.

title: Troponin Interpretation
content: High-sensitivity troponin (hs-cTnI/hs-cTnT): detects MI with >95% sensitivity. Delta troponin: rise ≥3-6ng/L in 1-2 hours or ≥5ng/L in 3 hours = NSTEMI. Single undetectable hs-cTn at 0h with low GRACE score = safe discharge. Causes of elevated troponin: demand ischemia, PE, myocarditis, HF, renal failure, sepsis, cardiac contusion.

title: Chest CT Interpretation - Lung Nodules
content: Fleischner Society guidelines: <6mm low-risk: no follow-up needed. 6-8mm: CT at 6-12 months. >8mm: CT at 3 months, PET-CT, or tissue sampling. High-risk features: spiculated margins, upper lobe location, prior malignancy, smoking history. Ground glass opacity (GGO) vs solid: GGO slower growth. Brock and Mayo model for malignancy risk.
